channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: FTC Accuses GoDaddy of Misleading Customers on Security
item.description: Federal Trade Commission (FTC) accuses GoDaddy of misleading customers and failing to protect its web hosting services sufficiently.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: ExpressVPN upgrades to post-quantum encryption NIST standards
item.description: Yet again, the VPN provider consolidates its commitment to future-proofing user data against the threats posed by quantum computing. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Google ups Workspace price, makes Gemini AI features available for free
item.description: Google will no longer charge Workspace customers an additional fee for Gemini, expanding access to generative AI (genAI) features in apps such as Gmail, Docs, and Sheets. At the same time, the company is increasing the price of Workspace subscriptions by a small amount.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ante la inminente llegada de la IA general, los CIO mantienen el rumbo de sus alianzas
item.description: La brecha entre la innovación en inteligencia artificial (IA) y el uso empresarial de la tecnología se está ampliando, pero eso no debería afectar la trayectoria de las estrategias de IA corporativa de los CIO actuales, dicen los expertos.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC posts 57% surge in profit thanks to the AI boom
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Taiwan computer chip maker TSMC reported Thursday that its profit in the last quarter rose 57%, buoyed by the artificial intelligence boom. The world’s biggest semiconductor manufacturer reported a net profit of 374.7 billion new Taiwan dollars ($11.4 billion), higher than expected.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Biden to sign executive order boosting digital identity protection
item.description: President Biden will sign an executive order on Thursday intended to boost the privacy of Americans amid continued cyberattacks against the U.S. "Adversary countries and criminals have increasingly targeted the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: It’s time for Apple to press pause on its AI news summaries
item.description: Apple should shut off its AI summaries of news alerts until it can prevent them from spreading fake news. Anecdotes of iPhone users seeing weirdly phrased summaries of texts and other content are common. But getting news headlines wrong is another matter.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Kazakhstan’s AI-Powered Prisons: Setting a New Standard for Safety and Efficiency
item.description: Technology innovations have made the digital transformation of public services essential to enhancing safety, efficiency, and accountability.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Red Hat offers OpenShift Virtualization Engine, Kubernetes Connectivity Link
item.description: Red Hat has made both its Red Hat OpenShift Virtualization Engine and Red Hat Connectivity Link software generally available. The company also announced Red Hat Advanced Cluster Management for Virtualization.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google signs deal with AP to deliver up-to-date news through its Gemini AI chatbot
item.description: Google says its artificial intelligence chatbot Gemini will now deliver up-to-date news from The Associated Press in the tech giant’s first such deal with a news publisher.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What Nokia was thinking when Apple introduced iPhone in 2007
item.description: The whole world was watching when legendary Apple CEO Steve Jobs launched the iconic iPhone in 2007. Mobile was the future. Apple knew it (and rebranded itself to reflect it). And Nokia had a fight on its hands. So, what did Nokia think about the iPhone launch? Now we know.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Musk may get office space in Eisenhower building: NYT
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk is expected to get an office space in the building adjacent to the White House, as he prepares to take the helm of President-elect Trump’s newly created Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), according to a New York Times report on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: What the tech industry wants from the Trump administration and Congress
item.description: The technology industry is calling on the incoming Trump administration and Congress to create the necessary tax and regulatory environment. The post What the tech industry wants from the Trump administration and Congress first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Biden signs ambitious order to bolster energy resources for AI data centers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Joe Biden on Tuesday signed an ambitious executive order on artificial intelligence that seeks to ensure the infrastructure needed for advanced AI operations, such as large-scale data centers and new clean power facilities, can be built quickly and at scale in the United
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Court lets Tennessee porn site age verification law take effect as Texas law goes to Supreme Court
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A panel of appeals judges has ruled that Tennessee can begin enforcing a law that requires pornographic websites to verify their visitors’ age as the First Amendment debate rises to the U.S. Supreme Court this week for oral arguments over a similar Texas law.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US TikTok users flock to Chinese app Xiaohongshu in protest with TikTok ban looming
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — As the threat of a TikTok ban looms, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Inside the DHS Science and Technology Directorate’s search for better wireless communications
item.description: The Homeland Security Department's Science and Technology Directorate has an ongoing research program for the development of cellular. The post Inside the DHS Science and Technology Directorate’s search for better wireless communications first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Hackers claim massive breach of company that tracks and sells Americans' location data
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals that hackers stole data from Gravy Analytics, a major seller of smartphone location data to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: First 15 things to do or try first when you get a new iPhone
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson: If you just bought a new iPhone, here’s what to do first when you fire it up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: For Microsoft, 2025 could be a game-changing year
item.description: It’s looking like 2025 will be one of the most consequential years for Microsoft in a long time. Since Satya Nadella became CEO in early 2014, the company has been on an upward trajectory, despite a few bumps along the way.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Brazil law restricts use of smartphones in elementary and high schools
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil's President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Monday signed a bill restricting the use of smartphones at school, following a global trend for such limitations. The move will impact students at elementary and high schools across the South American nation starting in February.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE and DIARLEI RODRIGUES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 5-minute cleanup for your phone and computer
item.description: Technology expert Kim Komando shares her easy tips on how to declutter your phones, computers and devices from the files you no longer need.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: CULVER CITY, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams defense appeared to be content to drop back in coverage and prevent San Francisco’s Brock Purdy from completing a Hail Mary on the final play of their Thursday night game on Dec. 12. The broadcast on Amazon Prime Video saw it differently, though.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KRON4
